query: 从新review下测试脚本， 没有问题的话，就要分析为什么没有明显回落，分析原因，找到解决方案；然后解决掉；

transcript.content: 对。如果把“project 层 working-set 变薄”当成“最终产品目标已达成”，那就是在骗自己。你的最终目标写得很清楚：**用户体感要明显变化，正常使用时不需要 `compact`**。按这个标准，当前 Codex 这条路径还没过。 **可落文案** 可以直接放进文档：
现在openclaw的实现来看，是否从实现思路上 已经达到了 控制整个propmt？
结论：**从实现思路上，OpenClaw 已经达到了“控制 prompt 主干”的位置；但还没达到“控制宿主最终整个 prompt 的全部组成”。** 更准确地说，OpenClaw 现在控制的是这三块：
那openclaw 长对话 context 最大的部分 是不是已经被接管了呢？
结论：**大概率是，OpenClaw 长对话里最大的那块动态 context，已经被接管了。** 原因很直接：
ok，当前的问题汇总下，写入到开发日志中； 完成后我们开始对openclaw进行测试；

当前问题汇总已经写入开发日志：[2026-04-19-clarify-host-level-context-minor-gc-acceptance-and-openclaw-feasibility.md](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/docs/devlog/2026-04-19-clarify-host-level-context-minor-gc-acceptance-and…
你刚才验证后，openclaw 是否达到了我们认为的用户端明显可用么/？ 整体的context 大小有明显的下降么？ （切不同话题）
还不能这么说。按我们刚才真正验证到的东西，答案是： - `OpenClaw 还不能判定为“用户端已经明显可用”` - `也没有证据证明整体 context 在切不同话题后有明显下降`

我们现在的任务是 深入的测试openclaw的 gc，是否能够达标；，尽量更长的对话（可以做临时数据写入，但是不要对我其他文档做写入操作）；做好探针，记录好相关数据；预期是：更长的对话，如果正常情况下，可能要做compact了，但是因为又了gc，实际不需要compact；同时在一个同一话题的长对话后切换话题，应该有明显的context回落；这里的context指的是实际发给llm的数据； 接下来你来策划这个测试，把相关配置都做好，然后就开始测试吧，最终简明扼要的给我结果和分析；

结论先说：**OpenClaw 现在已经能实打实地压住发给 LLM 的 prompt，但还没有达到“用户明显感觉切题后 context 明显回落、基本不用再考虑 compact”的标准。** 这轮我补了专门的长会话探针： - 脚本：[eval-openclaw-guarded-session-probe.js](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/scripts/eval-openc…
从新review下测试脚本， 没有问题的话，就要分析为什么没有明显回落，分析原因，找到解决方案；然后解决掉；

decision.archive_summary: Earlier discussion established that OpenClaw appears to control the main dynamic prompt block but had not yet shown clear user-visible context reduction or host-level full-prompt control. A devlog summary was written, then the task shifted to a concrete evaluation plan: test OpenClaw GC with longer conversations, add probes, avoid writing to unrelated user documents, measure the actual prompt sent to the LLM, and validate two expectations: long normal conversations should avoid needing compact, and switching topics after a long same-topic exchange should show a clear context drop. The assistant reported that prompt pressure was reduced in practice but not enough to meet the user-visible standard of obvious context fallback / no-compact confidence.
decision.reasoning_summary: The latest turn continues the active OpenClaw GC testing task, specifically asking to re-review the test script, diagnose the lack of obvious context rollback, find a fix, and implement it. Raw dependency is mainly the immediately prior task framing and result summary, so keep the recent active-task turns raw. Older conceptual Q&A, status checks, and the devlog handoff are resolved and can leave the next-turn working set. The user’s durable testing constraints from t10 should survive as a semantic pin even if later raw turns are trimmed.

applied.archiveSummary: Earlier discussion established that OpenClaw appears to control the main dynamic prompt block but had not yet shown clear user-visible context reduction or host-level full-prompt control. A devlog summary was written, then the task shifted to a concrete evaluation plan: test OpenClaw GC with longer conversations, add probes, avoid writing to unrelated user documents, measure the actual prompt sent to the LLM, and validate two expectations: long normal conversations should avoid needing compact, and switching topics after a long same-topic exchange should show a clear context drop. The assistant reported that prompt pressure was reduced in practice but not enough to meet the user-visible standard of obvious context fallback / no-compact confidence.

Raw dependency is mainly the immediately prior task framing and result summary, so keep the recent active-task turns raw. Older conceptual Q&A, status checks, and the devlog handoff are resolved and can leave the next-turn working set. The user’s durable testing constraints from t10 should survive as a semantic pin even if later raw turns are trimmed.
